organizations.role: PAC head / Executive Director (chair of the funding nonprofit)
organizations.notes: Filing lists occupation/employer as "SELF"/"CEO" (fields appear swapped, i.e. self-employed CEO) — a distinctive match to Michael Searle, an Austin political consultant who founded and runs Searle Strategies, a public-affairs firm, after serving as an aide to conservative Council Member Ellen Troxclair. He headed "Citizens for an Accountable Austin," the pro-Prop K PAC behind the 2018 city-efficiency-audit ballot measure, funded via roughly $137,000 from the dark-money nonprofit Austin Civic Fund, which he also led as Executive Director/chair; he declined to disclose the fund's donors, prompting a city Ethics Commission review of a possible dark-money-ordinance violation. Austin Chronicle describes him as a leader of ALEC's (American Legislative Exchange Council) local-government affiliate, which seeks partners to privatize municipal services. He also serves on the boards of the Austin Independent Business Alliance and Just Liberty (bipartisan criminal-justice reform) and on the City of Austin Tourism Commission. Identity confidence is medium: the CEO/self-employed occupation pairing is a strong, distinctive match to this public figure, but the Dripping Springs (78620) address was not independently corroborated in the sources found.
